message:
fix: 6 single_source_* + bidirectional_dijkstra unhashable parity (br-r37-c1-ybw1s)

Fifth follow-up to the unhashable-node series (br-r37-c1-m0io3 →
-g438p → -i9whv → -c4agn). Seven more node-parameter functions
raised the wrong exception class on unhashable inputs:

  single_source_dijkstra_path           fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_dijkstra_path_length    fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_dijkstra                fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford_path       fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford_path_length fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford            fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  bidirectional_dijkstra                fnx: TypeError     nx: NodeNotFound  ← REVERSE

The first six all need hash-validation up front to raise nx-shaped
TypeError. ``bidirectional_dijkstra`` is the inverse case: nx's
impl uses ``source not in G`` (silent-False on unhashable, no
hash op) and raises NodeNotFound; fnx delegates to dijkstra_path
which now hash-validates (after br-r37-c1-c4agn) and would raise
TypeError. Pre-membership-check source / target with the (silent-
False-on-unhashable) ``in`` operator and raise NodeNotFound for
any non-member input — matching nx's exact contract.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

- 6 single_source_* functions: add ``hash(source)`` at the top.

- ``bidirectional_dijkstra``: pre-check ``source not in G`` and
  ``target not in G`` (which silently swallow TypeError on
  unhashable, returning False) BEFORE delegating to dijkstra_path.
  Raise NodeNotFound matching nx's exact wording.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_unhashable_single_source_paths_parity.py``:
- test_single_source_unhashable_raises_typeerror (parametrized
  over 6 functions × {list, set, dict} = 18 cases)
- test_bidirectional_dijkstra_unhashable_raises_node_not_found
  (parametrized over unhashable types; source side)
- test_bidirectional_dijkstra_unhashable_target_raises_node_not_found
- 3 regression guards on hashable inputs

Full sweep
----------
6962 passed, 0 failed (was 6935 before — +27 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -166,6 +166,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xsrkr","title":"parity: Graph instances are content-equal and unhashable, nx uses identity-equal and hashable","description":"fnx.Graph defines content-based __eq__ in the Rust binding (path_graph(3) == path_graph(3) returns True) and is unhashable (Python sets __hash__ = None when __eq__ is defined). nx uses object's default identity-based __eq__ and __hash__: nx.path_graph(3) == nx.path_graph(3) returns False, nx.Graph() is hashable for use in sets/dict keys. Drop-in code that puts graphs in sets/dicts (set([G1, G2])) raises TypeError on fnx; code that compares graph identity (if G is reference_graph or G == reference_graph) gets opposite answers on fnx vs nx. Fix: override __eq__ and __hash__ on all four Graph classes to use object's defaults (identity-based) matching nx exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:42:25.251403517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.218447403Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.216475861Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 64e1627. Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph now use object's identity-based __eq__ and __hash__ matching nx exactly. set/dict containing graphs now works on fnx. 29 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xy4xf","title":"parity: branching/arborescence functions coerce int weights to float","description":"fnx.maximum_branching, fnx.minimum_branching, fnx.maximum_spanning_arborescence, fnx.minimum_spanning_arborescence on directed graphs return edge dicts with weight values coerced to float. nx preserves the original weight type (int weights stay int). Drop-in code that asserts isinstance(d['weight'], int) breaks. Default weight (1, when no edge has the weight attr) is also affected — fnx returns 1.0, nx returns 1. Root: Rust binding coerces to f64. Fix: after the Rust call, restore each retained edge's attribute dict from the source graph so the caller sees the original int (or whatever) type.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:01:54.571417216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:06:01.896383879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:06:01.894373329Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit bb2acaa. Branching/arborescence wrappers now preserve weight types from source matching nx. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-y8m6q","title":"write_weighted_edgelist and write_multiline_adjlist reject file-like objects","description":"fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(G, BytesIO()) and fnx.write_multiline_adjlist(G, BytesIO()) raise TypeError('expected str, bytes or os.PathLike object, not BytesIO'). nx accepts file-like objects (BytesIO) for these functions via the @open_file decorator and writes the encoded bytes directly.

Repro:
  >>> import io
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 2, {'weight': 1.5})])
  >>> nx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # OK, writes b'1 2 1.5\n'
  >>> fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # TypeError

Root cause: python/franken_networkx/readwrite/__init__.py:1099 (write_weighted_edgelist) and :998 (write_multiline_adjlist) blindly call open(path, 'w', ...) which only accepts file paths. write_edgelist (line 287, _write_bytes) and write_pajek (line 746) already implement the dual-path handling. Apply the same pattern.

Drop-in code that streams to BytesIO (very common in tests, network handlers, in-memory pipelines) silently breaks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:49:03.645856336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:55:37.775071124Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:55:37.772318030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-ybw1s","title":"single_source_dijkstra/bellman_ford raise wrong class on unhashable; bidirectional_dijkstra too strict","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-c4agn. Seven more node-parameter functions raise the wrong exception class on unhashable inputs:

  single_source_dijkstra_path           fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_dijkstra_path_length    fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_dijkstra                fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford_path       fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford_path_length fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  single_source_bellman_ford            fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  bidirectional_dijkstra                fnx: TypeError     nx: NodeNotFound  ← REVERSE

The first six all need hash-validation up front (matching nx's TypeError).

bidirectional_dijkstra is the inverse case — fnx is too strict. nx's bidirectional_dijkstra calls source not in G (which silently returns False on unhashable, no hash op) and raises NodeNotFound. fnx delegates to dijkstra_path which now hash-validates (after br-r37-c1-c4agn) and raises TypeError. To match nx, fnx must catch the TypeError and re-raise as NodeNotFound — or pre-membership-check before dispatching to dijkstra_path.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T10:30:49.762873021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T10:31:20.635123654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-yf4tf","title":"parity: NetworkXNotImplemented messages diverge from nx's standard 'not implemented for <type> type' format","description":"Three functions raise NetworkXNotImplemented with custom messages instead of nx's standard format: (1) immediate_dominators raises 'immediate_dominators is not defined for undirected graphs.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) is_strongly_connected raises 'is_strongly_connected is not defined for undirected graphs. Use is_connected instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (3) is_weakly_connected raises similar custom message vs nx's standard. Drop-in code that asserts on the exact NetworkXNotImplemented message text breaks. Fix: align messages to nx's @not_implemented_for decorator format ('not implemented for <type> type').","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:23:41.000065082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:26:14.013568279Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:26:14.011015876Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 7879d79. is_strongly_connected, is_weakly_connected, and immediate_dominators now raise NetworkXNotImplemented with nx's standard 'not implemented for <type> type' message. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ygr0e","title":"parity: node_classification harmonic_function + local_and_global_consistency wrappers (2 functions)","description":"networkx.algorithms.node_classification exposes two label-propagation classifiers that franken_networkx does not surface: harmonic_function(G, max_iter=30, label_name=label) and local_and_global_consistency(G, alpha=0.99, max_iter=30, label_name=label). Both are graph-based semi-supervised learning algorithms. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py via _call_networkx_submodule_for_parity(algorithms.node_classification, ...), export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:49:44.313573017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:06:42.170412280Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:06:42.168295571Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3106ac9: harmonic_function + local_and_global_consistency wrappers; both verified on labeled chain to return identical classifications to nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-yrj7i","title":"parity: generate_random_paths defaulted weight=None and ignored edge weights","description":"fnx.generate_random_paths(G, sample_size) defaulted weight=None vs nx weight='weight'. Worse, the implementation always did a uniform random.choice over neighbors regardless of the weight kwarg — even if weight='weight' was passed, edge weights were ignored. Concrete reproducer: G with edges (0,1,w=10) and (0,2,w=1); fnx.generate_random_paths(G, 500, path_length=1, source=0, seed=42, weight='weight') visited nodes 1 and 2 roughly equally; nx visits 1 ~10x more often. Fixed both: aligned default to 'weight' and rewrote the walk to call random.choices(nbrs, weights=...) when weight is set, falling back to uniform when weight=None. MultiGraph aggregates parallel-edge weights.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:12.416280200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:58.963972417Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:58.962095164Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 6f21573. weight='weight' now matches nx default; implementation honours weights via random.choices. 9 parity tests in test_generate_random_paths_parity.py freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+    hash(source)
     # br-dijkignoreweight: the Rust single_source_dijkstra silently
     # returns hop-count distances on any weighted input (it ignores
     # the weight attribute). Delegate to nx when any edge carries a
@@ -10294,6 +10296,8 @@ def single_source_dijkstra(G, source, target=None, cutoff=None, weight="weight")

     # br-dijkignoreweight: same as single_source_dijkstra.
     if _should_delegate_dijkstra_to_networkx(G, weight) or _graph_has_nonunit_weight(G, weight):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
@@ -10304,6 +10308,8 @@ def single_source_dijkstra_path(G, source, cutoff=None, weight="weight"):
 
 
 def single_source_dijkstra_path_length(G, source, cutoff=None, weight="weight"):
+    # br-r37-c1-ybw1s: nx-shaped TypeError on unhashable source.
+    hash(source)
     # br-dijkignoreweight: delegate weighted inputs to nx.
     if _should_delegate_dijkstra_to_networkx(G, weight) or _graph_has_nonunit_weight(G, weight):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
@@ -10318,6 +10324,8 @@ def single_source_dijkstra_path_length(G, source, cutoff=None, weight="weight"):
 
 
 def single_source_bellman_ford(G, source, target=None, weight="weight"):
+    # br-r37-c1-ybw1s: nx-shaped TypeError on unhashable source.
+    hash(source)
     # br-bfignoreweight: same weight-ignoring bug as dijkstra — the
     # Rust Bellman-Ford returns hop-count distances on weighted input.
     # Delegate any weighted graph to nx.
@@ -10342,6 +10350,8 @@ def single_source_bellman_ford(G, source, target=None, weight="weight"):
 
 
 def single_source_bellman_ford_path(G, source, weight="weight"):
+    # br-r37-c1-ybw1s: nx-shaped TypeError on unhashable source.
+    hash(source)
     # br-bfignoreweight: delegate weighted inputs.
     if _should_delegate_bellman_ford_to_networkx(weight) or _graph_has_nonunit_weight(G, weight):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
@@ -10356,6 +10366,8 @@ def single_source_bellman_ford_path(G, source, weight="weight"):
 
 
 def single_source_bellman_ford_path_length(G, source, weight="weight"):
+    # br-r37-c1-ybw1s: nx-shaped TypeError on unhashable source.
+    hash(source)
     # br-bfignoreweight: delegate weighted inputs.
     if _should_delegate_bellman_ford_to_networkx(weight) or _graph_has_nonunit_weight(G, weight):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
@@ -15518,6 +15530,18 @@ def bidirectional_dijkstra(G, source, target, weight="weight"):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity(
             "bidirectional_dijkstra", G, source, target, weight=weight
         )
+    # br-r37-c1-ybw1s: nx's bidirectional_dijkstra checks
+    # ``source not in G`` (which silently returns False on
+    # unhashable, no hash op) and raises NodeNotFound — *not*
+    # TypeError. fnx's local fallback delegates to dijkstra_path
+    # which now hash-validates (br-r37-c1-c4agn) and raises
+    # TypeError. Mirror nx's contract: pre-membership-check
+    # source / target with the (silent-False-on-unhashable) ``in``
+    # operator and raise NodeNotFound for any non-member input.
+    if source not in G:
+        raise NodeNotFound(f"Source {source} is not in G")
+    if target not in G:
+        raise NodeNotFound(f"Target {target} is not in G")
     path = dijkstra_path(G, source, target, weight=weight)
     length = dijkstra_path_length(G, source, target, weight=weight)
     return (length, path)

@@ -0,0 +1,123 @@
+"""Parity for single_source_* and bidirectional_dijkstra on unhashable.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-ybw1s (fifth follow-up to the unhashable-node series:
+br-r37-c1-m0io3 → -g438p → -i9whv → -c4agn). Six single_source_*
+functions raised the wrong exception class (NodeNotFound) instead
+of nx's TypeError; bidirectional_dijkstra had the OPPOSITE drift
+(TypeError instead of nx's NodeNotFound).
+
+Functions affected:
+  single_source_dijkstra_path           fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  single_source_dijkstra_path_length    fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  single_source_dijkstra                fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  single_source_bellman_ford_path       fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  single_source_bellman_ford_path_length fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  single_source_bellman_ford            fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
+  bidirectional_dijkstra                fnx: TypeError     nx: NodeNotFound
+
+The first six all hash-check up front. ``bidirectional_dijkstra``
+needs the *opposite* fix: nx's impl uses ``source not in G``
+(silent-False on unhashable, no hash op) and raises NodeNotFound;
+fnx must pre-membership-check before delegating to dijkstra_path
+(which now hash-validates and would raise TypeError).
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+UNHASHABLE = [
+    pytest.param([1, 2], id="list"),
+    pytest.param({1, 2}, id="set"),
+    pytest.param({"a": 1}, id="dict"),
+]
+
+SS_FNS = [
+    "single_source_dijkstra_path",
+    "single_source_dijkstra_path_length",
+    "single_source_dijkstra",
+    "single_source_bellman_ford_path",
+    "single_source_bellman_ford_path_length",
+    "single_source_bellman_ford",
+]
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("fn_name", SS_FNS)
+def test_single_source_unhashable_raises_typeerror(fn_name, val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    f_fn = getattr(fnx, fn_name)
+    n_fn = getattr(nx, fn_name)
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        f_fn(G, val)
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        n_fn(GX, val)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_bidirectional_dijkstra_unhashable_raises_node_not_found(val):
+    """nx's bidirectional_dijkstra uses ``source not in G`` (which
+    silently returns False on unhashable, no hash op) and raises
+    NodeNotFound. fnx must match — pre-membership-check before
+    delegating to dijkstra_path which would otherwise raise
+    TypeError (after br-r37-c1-c4agn)."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NodeNotFound, match=r"Source .* is not in G"):
+        fnx.bidirectional_dijkstra(G, val, 1)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NodeNotFound, match=r"Source .* is not in G"):
+        nx.bidirectional_dijkstra(GX, val, 1)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_bidirectional_dijkstra_unhashable_target_raises_node_not_found(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NodeNotFound):
+        fnx.bidirectional_dijkstra(G, 1, val)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NodeNotFound):
+        nx.bidirectional_dijkstra(GX, 1, val)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression — hashable / missing inputs unaffected
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_single_source_missing_hashable_still_node_not_found():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NodeNotFound):
+        fnx.single_source_dijkstra_path(G, 99)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NodeNotFound):
+        nx.single_source_dijkstra_path(GX, 99)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_single_source_dijkstra_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.single_source_dijkstra_path(G, 1)
+    n = nx.single_source_dijkstra_path(GX, 1)
+    assert dict(f) == dict(n)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_bidirectional_dijkstra_hashable_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+    assert fnx.bidirectional_dijkstra(G, 1, 4) == nx.bidirectional_dijkstra(GX, 1, 4)
